titleStmt.title: National Library of Sweden, A 199
titleStmt.sponsor: Medieval Latin Manuscripts in the National Library: Cataloguing and Digitization
titleStmt.funder: Riksbankens jubileumsfond – The Swedish Foundation for Humanities and Social Sciences

publicationStmt.publisher: manuscripta.se: A Digital Catalogue of Medieval and Early Modern Manuscripts in Sweden

msDesc.head: Henricus de Frimaria, Tractatus de decem preceptis; Expositio salutationis angelicae. Thomas ab Aquino, De rationibus fidei

p.note: : the flyleaf has been removed but the text on it has left an offprint.

supportDesc.foliation: Modern foliation in pencil (from the foliation has been corrected). ad deum in montem

collation.p: VisColl collation. Structure of quire 10 tentative, most likely it originally conisted of 8 leaves, but three of the remaining leaves are today almost detached. Parchment guards at the centre of each quire, and between first and second quire.
collation.catchwords: : Catchwords written inside a single or double frame with red ink strike-through.

p.note: Upper left corner, National Library book label.

Top of page, in black ink by a medieval hand: Ad carthusienses prope erffordiam; right margin, written perpendicular to the previous text: Theodericus de luneborg leuoldi procurator; the pastedown also contains some scribblings or pen-trials by a medieval hand.

binding.p: Medieval binding. Red leather over bevelled wooden boards. 4 double raised bands and endbands. Brass hook and clasp. , in ink by a medieval hand on a parchment label: Super x precepta exposicio Henrici de Frimaria Exposicio bona valde super dominicam oracionem et angelicam salutacionem Thomas de racionibus fidei Methodius et cetera; beneath, traces of a removed label. , traces of a removed label; fragments of an old label; stamped in gold: A 199.

: pastedown consist of a fragment from a theological manuscript. The side facing out is blank, but the leaf is partially detached from the cover and text on the other side is visible in the lower left corner. The same leaf is visible on .
binding.condition: Leather worn and very faded. Boards exposed at corners. Endbands exposed. The is full of holes from bookworms.

origin.p: Germany , 15th century. The manuscript carries owner's notes by the Carthusians in Erfurt, who may also have written the manuscript.

provenance.p: According to the note on , , and the manuscript belonged to the Carthusians in Erfurt.

acquisition.p: Purchased by the National Library from George Stephens in .

summary.p: The manuscript is missing the last quires. If the medieval list of content on is correct the manuscript is missing around 10 texts.

msItem.title: De decem preceptis
msItem.incipit: Audi israel precepta domini et ea in corde tuo (Deu. 4:1) In verbis propositis spiritus sanctus circa diuina precepta tria tangit
msItem.explicit: quod vinum nobis meritis gloriose virginis prestare dignetur eius filius benedictus qu cum patre et spiritu sancto est vnus deus benedictus in secula seculorum amen

msItem.title: Table of content
msItem.incipit: Vt autem in isto tractatu contenta legenti promptius occurant hinc est quod puncta nobiliora in presenti tabula seriatim tulis designare
msItem.explicit: Explicit tabula super tractatum decem preceptorum editum pro instructionem simplicium ad laudem dei et profectum animarum Cui sit honor et gloria per infinita secula seculorum Amen

note.p: The table of content is introduced by a short introduction, which is then followed by a list of chapters and subchapters keyed to the text with letters that are found in the margin of the text.

p.note: Parts of one or two large watermarks visible on the upper part of the leaves, e.g. and .

supportDesc.foliation: : contemporary foliation 1-54, and some sort of chapter markers with letters and Roman numerals.

handDesc.handNote: Gothic cursiva antiquior (both looped and unlooped ascenders occur).

p.note: Small paper note stored in a modern envelope. The text is a list of content written by a medieval hand: hic liber est fratrum carthusiensium prope erffordiam Contenta henricus de frimaria circa X precepta cum triplici sensu cuiuslibet cum tabula in fine eiusdem tractatus Exposicio bona dominice oracionis Similiter super angelicam salutacionem secundum sensum literalem et misticam Et videntur ambe eiusdem henrici de Frimaria Tractatus beati Thome de Racionibus fidei ad cantorem Anthiocenum Aliqua capitula pulcra de sancta trinitate et de verbo patris Sumpta ex libro beato Thome de fide spe et caritate .
Same hans as on the recto: hic liber est fratrum carthusiensium prope Erffordiam Contenta hic (the last two words smudged or erased).

Top margin (to the right of the rubric Prologus): Hic sunt hec de decem preceptis cum tabula sua in fine Item Exposicio super pater noster bona Amen Item Exposicio super Ave maria Vrimariensis Item tractatus beati thome de racionibus fidei (to the left of Prologus with a signe-de-renvoi): Idem de fide spe et caritate Item liber de adam et eua Item de david Item de nativitate marie et assumpcione eius Oracio de corpore Christi Item Methodius liber eius Item sermo de nativitate christi Item de ultimo iudicio et de 15 signis et de antichristo Item de sancto paulo (below the phrase de primo with a signe-de-renvoi:) apostolo scilicet eius conversione Item casus episcopales et aliorum Item de Confessione et cetera Item de vestibus sacerdotalibus ?et alia scil. super? Missam albertus magnus . Bottom margin, in pencil by a modern hand: Cartusiae Erfordiensis; below, in ink by a modern hand: inköpt till Kgl. Bibl. fr G. Stephens 23/10 1848.
Framed tituli and keywords by the scribe in the marigins and marginal additions by at least one contemporary hand.

decoDesc.p: Main text in brown ink, capitals touched in red, lemma underlined in red.

decoNote.p: : plain initials over 2-4 lines in red.
, , , , : bookmarks in red paper glued to the page.

msItem.title: Exposicio oracionis dominice
msItem.incipit: PAter noster Hinc dominice oracioni beatus gregorius premittit prefacionem
msItem.explicit: qui cum patre et spiritu sancto viuit et regnat deus gloriosus per infinita secula seculorum amen

msItem.title: Excerpt from The Sentences
msItem.rubric: Explicit exposicio liber primus sententiarum Distinctio Ia
msItem.incipit: Augustinus de doctrina christiana liber primus

note.p: Excerpts from Distincio 1-3 of the Sentences, Book 1.

msItem.title: Expositio salutationis angelice
msItem.rubric: Incipit exposicio salutacionis angelice secundum duplicem sensum litteralem videlicet et misticum edita per magistrum de vrymaria
msItem.incipit: Qvia oracionem dominice secundum ritum ecclesie et deuocionem fidelium
msItem.explicit: Quam desponsacionem nobis patrare? dignetur qui est benedictus in secula seculorum amen
msItem.finalRubric: Et in hoc terminatur exposicio salutacionis angelice ad honorem dei et gloriose virginis matris eius marie

p.note: Parts of at least one type of watermark visible on the inner edge of the leaves, e.g. on .

supportDesc.condition: Leaves stained and ink smudged in places. : large red ink stain in the outer margin.

handDesc.handNote: Gothic cursiva antiquior (both looped and unlooped ascenders occur).

p.note: Additions by one or several contemporary hands in the margin. The additions consist of corrections, comments, and nota-marks.
Additions framed in red triangels by the scribe in the margins. The additions consist of summaries or key-words from the text. There are also additions by a second hand, e.g. on .

decoDesc.p: Main text in black-brown ink, capitals touched in red, rubrics in red.

decoNote.p: : text opens with plain initials over 4 lines in red.
: plain initials over 2-3 lines in red.
: text opens with plain initials in red with some penwork over 5 lines.
: plain initials over 3-4 lines in red.
: bookmarks in red paper glued to the page.

msItem.title: De Rationibus Fidei ad Cantorem Antiochenum
msItem.rubric: Incipit tractatus sancti doctoris fratris thome de aquino ordinis predicatorum ad Cantorem antiocenum de racionibus fidei
msItem.incipit: Beatus petrus apostolus qui promissionem accepit ut super eius confessionem fundaretur ecclesia

msItem.title: Excerpts from De fide
msItem.incipit: BEatus thomas in libro de fide spe et caritate ad fratrem reynaldum capitulo 13a
msItem.explicit: Latini dicunt tres personas greci vero tres ypostasis patrem et filium et spiritum sanctum et cetera
msItem.note: The text consist of De fide, chapter 38-50.

msItem.title: Treatise on the triangle and the Trinity
msItem.rubric: Ad g-s Nota exemplum trine vnitatis de triangulo
msItem.incipit: In figura vero relucet beate trinitatis similitudo
msItem.explicit: ipsi sint eadem superficies et sigillatim vnusquisque et in summo esse et cetera

p.note: Parts of one type of watermark, most likely a tower, visible on the inner edge of the leaves, e.g. .

supportDesc.condition: Leaves dirty and partially or fully detached from the binding. Several quires have been removed at the end, which has caused a break or gap in the inside of the spine. A booklet made of modern paper has been added for support (visible on ).

handDesc.handNote: Gothic cursive. The text on these leaves varies a great deal in terms of ink and the size of the script. It may be written by several different hands, but it is also possible that it is the same scribe using different pens.

p.note: Additions in the margins by at least two different contemporary hands. The additions consist of corrections, phrases such as Nota bene on , and comments on the state of the text, e.g. , bottom margin: hic ulla/-us deficit.

decoDesc.p: Main text in brown ink, capitals touched in red, rubrics underlined in red.

decoNote.p: : plain initials over 2-3 lines in red.
: space reserved for initials, often with guide letters.
: bookmarks in red paper glued to the page.
